title: East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 5.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.33: East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.33' running on pan:traffic with src_zone, dest_zone, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup ot_zone_pairs.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed_conduit
| where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| sort - bytes
description: Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes fw and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain zone pair matrix from ISA-62443 drawings; (2) log intra-zone noise baseline; (3) alert new apps; (4) feed exceptions with expiry. Indexes required: `index=fw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`; Lookups: `ot_zone_pairs`, `ot_zone_pairs.csv`.
visualization: Sankey (zone flows), Table, Single value (new pairs/week). Key fields: `app`, `bytes`, `dest_zone`, `src_zone`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain zone pair matrix from ISA-62443 drawings; (2) log intra-zone noise baseline; (3) alert new apps; (4) feed exceptions with expiry.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup ot_zone_pairs.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed_conduit
| where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| sort - bytes
```

Understanding this SPL

**East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.1)** — Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: fw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=fw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_zone, dest_zone, app** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.1)** — Detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Palo Alto (2757), NetFlow. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Sankey (zone flows), Table, Single value (new pairs/week).
grandmaExplanation: We detects flows between OT zones that should communicate only through defined conduits, evidencing segmentation policy. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Network_Traffic data model (Network_Traffic.All_Traffic).
